puzzle0.title: SIGNAL TUNING
puzzle0.description: Tune the emergency frequency until Maya's unstable survivor transmission locks.

event10.text: Not a code. Not a protocol. Just something a person would say.

event12.text: My relay is drifting and I can't hold this channel for long.

choice0.text: TELL HER YOU CAN HEAR HER.

event3.text: Okay, that's a voice. That's a real answer.

event6.text: I forgot how much that matters.

event8.text: I can't give you everything yet. The signal keeps tearing at the edges.

event10.text: Keep me on the channel and I'll tell you who I am.

event3.text: Annoying.

event7.text: Fine. Verify me. I would verify me too.

event9.text: I've been alone long enough to stop trusting anything that answers too quickly.

event0.title: EMERGENCY AUDIO TRANSCRIPT
event0.content:
CHANNEL: 7.14 MHz
SIGNAL STATE: DISTORTED
VOICEPRINT: UNKNOWN FEMALE
RECOVERED FRAGMENTS:

"...anyone..."
"...relay is drifting..."
"...not Hale..."
"...lower access spine..."

Recommended action: manual signal tuning required.
event0.caption: Text reconstruction of distorted emergency audio from channel 7.14.

event6.text: The carrier keeps sliding under me. It feels like trying to stand on a floor that forgot it was solid.

event8.text: If you can tune it, do it now.

event10.text: If you wait, I go back to shouting into static.

event1.text: Do not let that channel lead.

event3.text: Tune it if you have to. But keep control of the carrier.

event5.text: If she gives you a name, don't assume the name makes her safe.

event7.text: Names are easy. Signals lie better than people.

event9.text: And yes, I know that includes me.

event3.text: No— don't let it drift.

event5.text: I had you for a second. Don't make me go back to static.

event7.text: Hold it steady when it bites. The drift gets worse right before the lock.

event1.text: Listen. Not to the words. To the pull.

event3.text: It drifts below seven, then snaps back. It wants you to overcorrect.

event5.text: Don't chase it. Let it come to 7.14 and hold there.

event7.text: I hate that I sound like Hale right now.

choice1.text: ASK HALE FOR A STABILIZATION TIP.

event3.text: Don't chase the carrier. Predict the drift. If the frequency is fighting you, it is probably trying to make you move too much.

event5.text: Small corrections. Hold on the target. Let the lock build.

event7.text: And don't tell her I helped. She will be insufferable about it.
I forgot how to talk to someone without checking if they were already dead.

event14.text: I was starting to think I'd die talking to static.

event16.text: I'm pinned beside a damaged relay console in the lower access spine.

event18.text: The console casing is cracked open. One support strut is bent across my left leg.

event20.text: I can move it, but not quietly. And not without using oxygen I don't have.

event22.text: And if Ethan Hale is on your other channel...

event0.title: SURVIVOR BIOMETRIC SCAN
event0.content:
CHANNEL SOURCE: 7.14 MHz
VOICEPRINT MATCH: MAYA CHEN
LIFE SIGNS: ACTIVE
OXYGEN: 18%
LOCATION ESTIMATE: DAMAGED RELAY CONSOLE / LOWER ACCESS SPINE
VISUAL FEED: UNAVAILABLE
STATUS: ALIVE / CRITICAL
event0.caption: Automated survivor verification generated from channel telemetry. No image recovered.

event9.text: Oxygen is at eighteen percent. It feels lower.

event11.text: The relay console is holding part of the debris off my leg.

event13.text: I can stay long enough to pull local telemetry, or I can move before my lungs start making decisions for me.

choice1.text: MOVE MAYA TOWARD THE ACCESS SPINE.

event3.text: I named it Malcolm.

event5.text: The cracked relay console. Don't judge me. You get lonely enough, everything with a blinking light starts having a personality.

event7.text: Malcolm is not my friend, but Malcolm is keeping debris off my leg, so we're being professional.

event9.text: I need both hands for this. If I keep talking, I'm going to drop the local buffer.

event3.text: Good. I was one bad thought away from arguing with Malcolm again.

event5.text: I'm going to pull myself clear and head for the access spine.

event7.text: If I swear, pretend the channel corrupted it.

event4.text: Of course that's the first thing he says.

event10.text: Operator, listen to me. Hale is very good at sounding reasonable while the room burns.

event17.text: He will make this about procedure. About logs. About proof.

event19.text: And while you're asking the clean questions, something down here keeps moving.

event21.text: I'm not asking you to trust me forever.

event23.text: I'm asking you to get me through the next door.

event4.text: I know that probably felt wrong.

event6.text: He makes silence feel like betrayal.

event8.text: I don't have much air, so I'm going to be blunt.

event10.text: Hale is dead. I am not saying that because I heard a rumor.

event12.text: I saw what was left of the corridor.

event14.text: And now I need to get through Service Lock C before whatever is using his signal notices I'm moving.

event3.text: But if he starts steering this conversation, I cut the channel myself.

event5.text: Technically you would interrupt the carrier.

event13.text: Operator, the longer both channels stay open, the uglier the carrier gets.

event15.text: Then help instead of narrating the problem.

event3.text: Left is a maintenance crawlway. Tight enough that my shoulders already hate it.

event5.text: Right is the main access spine. Faster. More open.

event7.text: There's a reflection in the panel glass on the right side.

event12.text: I hate reflections. They always know things first.

event3.text: Great. I love survival choices that involve becoming luggage.

event5.text: I'm going in head-first. Radio clipped to my collar.

event7.text: If the signal drops, it's because I'm dragging myself through a metal throat.

event3.text: Fast and stupid. Sometimes that works.

event5.text: I'm crossing now. Keeping the channel low.

event1.text: Service Lock C still has pressure.

event3.text: Barely. It keeps cycling like it can't decide whether to save me or make a point.

event5.text: There were six of us assigned to ECHO.

event7.text: Hale. Rourke. Lin. Patel. Arman. Me.

event11.text: Lin labeled everything, including things that were already labels.

event13.text: Patel pretended he hated everyone and fixed their coffee ratios when they weren't looking.

event15.text: Arman sang when he was nervous.

event18.text: He sang a lot near the end.

choice1.text: WHAT DO YOU NEED RIGHT NOW?

event1.text: You want proof. Good. Keep wanting proof.

event3.text: But understand this: the station is very good at handing you records after everyone who could explain them is gone.

event5.text: You will find logs. Timestamps. Clean little labels for ugly things.

event7.text: What you won't find is the sound Hale made when the corridor decompressed.

event12.text: If you need proof, get me to the service lock terminal.

event14.text: I can pull the survivor statement from my side.

event3.text: Air.

event7.text: After that, a room where nothing knows my name.

event9.text: Operationally: I need Service Lock C open without cycling the outer seal.

event11.text: Emotionally: ask me later. Preferably when my lungs stop voting against me.

event13.text: Get me to the terminal. I can send a statement and maybe keep myself alive.

event3.text: It's only a few meters. Which is a very long distance when every breath feels rented.
That took longer than it should have. The buffer kept dropping whenever I shifted my weight.

event5.text: My leg is not broken. I think. It hurts in a way that feels negotiable.

event6.title: MAYA RELAY TELEMETRY
event6.content:
LOWER ACCESS SPINE
OXYGEN BLEED: ACTIVE
AIRFLOW SOURCE: SERVICE LOCK C
SECONDARY MOVEMENT TRACE: DETECTED NEAR DOOR 12 RETURN LINE
NOTE: trace does not match Maya's current position.
event6.caption: Maya extracted this while stabilizing the damaged relay console.

event8.text: Movement trace near the Door 12 return line.

event12.text: So. Good news: we got data.

event14.text: Bad news: something else is moving.

event3.text: Not graceful. Not quiet. But clear.

event5.text: I'm in the access spine now.

event7.text: Narrow. Cold. The wall panels are sweating condensation.

event9.text: It smells like hot plastic and old blood.

event11.text: I don't have the telemetry from the console.

event13.text: But I'm farther from the oxygen leak.

event15.text: That's a trade I can live with. Literally.

event3.text: Slow. Quiet. Awful.

event5.text: There are scratch marks inside the paneling.

event9.text: Somebody tried to get through here in the dark.

event11.text: I'm going to pretend I didn't see that until my body has time to schedule a reaction.

event4.text: Something moved behind me halfway across.

event10.text: I hit my shoulder on a hatch frame and said a word that would get me banned from two mission briefings.

event12.text: But I'm here. Door is in front of me.

event3.text: Screen is cracked. Buttons are sticky. I am choosing not to identify the substance.

event4.title: SURVIVOR STATEMENT: MAYA
event4.content:
TEXT TRANSCRIPT / CHANNEL 7.14

MAYA CHEN:
"There were six assigned crew members on ECHO. After the breach, only one confirmed biological survivor remained: me. The signal using Ethan Hale's identity is not a living crew member. Do not allow it to control station routing."

Transcript confidence: 91%
event4.caption: Text transcript reconstructed from Maya's stabilized emergency channel.

event10.text: Like the station is politely avoiding the word alone.
